title: BERTIE BEETLE
content:
The all-time favourite BERTIE BEETLE is a bar of chocolate shaped like an anthropomorphised beetle with small pieces of honeycomb throughout. The Bertie Beetle is an Australian Chocolate bar manufactured by Nestle. The Bertie Beetle Do Not Contain Gelatine and is suitable for vegetarians.

Ingredients

Sugar, Vegetable Fat [Emulsifiers (Soy Lecithin, 4920], Milk Solids, Toffee Pieces (8%) – Sugar, Glucose Syrup (Derived from Wheat), Vegetable Fat, Raising Agent (Sodium Bicarbonate) – Cocoa Emulsifier (Soy Lecithin), Salt, Flavours. They do contain glucose from wheat. Bertie Beetle contain Milk, Soy and wheat. Manufactured on equipment that processes products containing peanuts and tree nuts.

Product Details

Product Of: Nestle Australia

Packing: Individually wrapped

Net Weight: 10g

Brand: Nestle

Why Choose Bertie Beetle?

Bertie Beetle is unique because it's shaped like an anthropomorphised beetle and contains small pieces of honeycomb throughout the chocolate, giving it a distinctive texture and fun appearance that's appealed to Australians for generations. If you're looking to stock up on this classic treat, you can find a full range of bulk lollies in Australia including Bertie Beetles for parties and events.

You may also want to buy

  • Bertie Beetle 10g$1.5
  • Bertie Beetle in the Show Bag10 pcs$15

Allergen Information

Bertie Beetle contains milk, soy, and wheat (in the glucose syrup). It is also manufactured on equipment that processes peanuts and tree nuts, so there may be traces of these allergens present. For those with dietary restrictions, it's important to check labels carefully. If you're looking for alternatives, we have a guide to gluten free lollies Australia and dairy free chocolate Australia for those with specific dietary needs.

Frequently Asked Questions

Is Bertie Beetle suitable for vegetarians?

Yes, Bertie Beetle is suitable for vegetarians as it does not contain gelatine. However, it does contain milk and soy, so it's not suitable for vegans.

What allergens does Bertie Beetle contain?

Bertie Beetle contains milk, soy, and wheat (in the glucose syrup). It is also manufactured on equipment that processes peanuts and tree nuts, so there may be traces of these allergens present.

What makes Bertie Beetle different from other chocolate bars?

Bertie Beetle is unique because it's shaped like an anthropomorphised beetle and contains small pieces of honeycomb throughout the chocolate, giving it a distinctive texture and fun appearance that's appealed to Australians for generations.

How much does one Bertie Beetle weigh?

Each Bertie Beetle bar weighs 10g and comes individually wrapped, making it a convenient single-serve chocolate treat.

Who manufactures Bertie Beetle and where?

Bertie Beetle is manufactured by Nestle Australia, one of the world's largest food and beverage companies, ensuring quality production of this beloved Australian chocolate bar.
